options.info: Display aspect ratio. Auto matches the game's native aspect; Stretch fills the window.
options.label: Aspect Ratio

options.info: Synchronizes output to the display refresh rate. Reduces tearing.
options.label: V-Sync

options.info: Uses high-resolution timers and busy-waiting for improved frame pacing. Slightly higher power use.
options.label: Precision Frame Timing

options.info: How shaders are compiled. Ubershader modes reduce stutter at a GPU cost; Skip Drawing is for debugging.
options.label: Shader Compilation

options.info: Pre-compile the shader pipeline before launching. Slower start, smoother first minutes of gameplay.
options.label: Compile Shaders Before Starting

options.info: Render scale relative to native (1x = 640x528; 6x is roughly 4K).
options.label: Internal Resolution

options.info: Reduces aliasing on edges. SSAA is far more demanding than MSAA but also anti-aliases shader effects.
options.label: Anti-Aliasing

options.info: Sharpens distant textures (anisotropic) and optionally forces a fixed magnification filter.
options.label: Texture Filtering

options.info: Algorithm used to resample the rendered image to the window size.
options.label: Output Resampling

options.info: 3D-stereoscopic rendering mode. Off disables stereo entirely.
options.label: Stereoscopic 3D Mode

options.info: Resize EFB copies to match the rendering scale. Required for high internal resolutions to look right.
options.label: Scaled EFB Copy

options.info: Higher-quality lighting at a small performance cost.
options.label: Per-Pixel Lighting

options.info: Force 4:3 games to render in widescreen by hacking the projection matrix. Can produce artifacts.
options.label: Widescreen Hack

options.info: Force higher-precision color output. Reduces banding on gradients.
options.label: Force 24-Bit Color

options.info: Skip rendering fog effects.
options.label: Disable Fog

options.info: Detect when a game uses mipmaps as separate images rather than true LODs.
options.label: Arbitrary Mipmap Detection

options.info: Disable the post-process copy-filter pass. Reduces blur some games apply.
options.label: Disable Copy Filter

options.info: Output in HDR when the display supports it.
options.label: HDR Post-Processing

options.info: Swap the left and right eye images.
options.label: Swap Eyes

options.info: Render each eye at the full internal resolution instead of half. Doubles GPU cost.
options.label: Full Resolution Per Eye

options.info: How aggressively cached textures are validated. Safe = fewest misses (most accurate), Fast = highest performance.
options.label: Texture Cache Accuracy

options.info: Ignore CPU reads/writes of the EFB. Speed boost; disables some EFB-based effects.
options.label: Skip EFB Access from CPU

options.info: Ignore EFB format changes. Speed win for many games; minor defects in a few.
options.label: Ignore Format Changes

options.info: Keep EFB copies on the GPU, bypassing RAM. Big speed boost; rare defects.
options.label: Store EFB Copies to Texture Only

options.info: Wait for GPU sync before writing EFB copies to RAM. Speed boost.
options.label: Defer EFB Copies to RAM

options.info: Decode textures on the GPU instead of the CPU.
options.label: GPU Texture Decoding

options.info: Keep XFB copies on the GPU. Big speed boost; rare defects.
options.label: Store XFB Copies to Texture Only

options.info: Display the XFB as soon as it's drawn. Lower latency, slight tearing risk.
options.label: Immediately Present XFB

options.info: Detect and skip identical consecutive frames to save GPU work.
options.label: Skip Presenting Duplicate Frames

options.info: Use a faster GPU-friendly depth calculation path.
options.label: Fast Depth Calculation

options.info: Disable bounding-box emulation. Big GPU speed-up; a few games need it (e.g. Paper Mario).
options.label: Disable Bounding Box

options.info: Round vertex coordinates to integers. Fixes seams in some games at high resolutions.
options.label: Vertex Rounding

options.info: Save the texture cache in save states. Larger states, smoother resume.
options.label: Save Texture Cache to State

options.info: Skip Vertical Blank Interrupts when lag is detected. Smoother audio off-100%; can freeze.
options.label: VBI Skip

options.info: Load high-resolution texture replacements from the user's Load/Textures folder.
options.label: Load Custom Textures

options.info: Pre-load all custom textures into VRAM at boot. Eliminates load stutter; uses more memory.
options.label: Prefetch Custom Textures

options.info: Load graphics mods from the user's Load/GraphicMods folder.
options.label: Enable Graphics Mods

options.info: Crop overscan/black borders from the rendered image.
options.label: Crop

options.info: Distribute video-backend work across multiple threads. Recommended on.
options.label: Backend Multithreading

options.info: Expand line/point primitives in the vertex shader instead of the geometry shader. Driver workaround.
options.label: Prefer VS for Point/Line Expansion

options.info: Cull invisible geometry on the CPU before sending to the GPU. Speeds up some games.
options.label: Cull Vertices on the CPU

options.info: Reduce overhead by deferring EFB-cache invalidations. Speed win; rare glitches.
options.label: Defer EFB Cache Invalidation

options.info: Trade some speed for accuracy in the texture sampler. (Checked = manual; disables fast sampling.)
options.label: Manual Texture Sampling

options.info: Point size for on-screen messages.
options.label: On-Screen Message Font Size

options.info: Sliding window for FPS/VPS averaging. Higher = more stable, slower to update.
options.label: Performance Sample Window

options.info: Display Dolphin's own status messages (save states, achievements, etc.).
options.label: Show On-Screen Messages

options.info: Frames per second the GPU is drawing.
options.label: Show FPS

options.info: Per-frame GPU time graph.
options.label: Show Frame Times

options.info: VBlanks per second — the rate the game thinks it's running at.
options.label: Show VPS

options.info: Per-vblank time graph.
options.label: Show VBlank Times

options.info: Emulation speed as a percentage of native.
options.label: Show % Speed

options.info: Render the FPS/VPS history as a graph.
options.label: Show Performance Graphs

options.info: Tint the speed indicator based on how close to native it is.
options.label: Show Speed Colors

options.info: How the audio DSP is emulated. HLE is fast and compatible; LLE is slower but accurate and required by a few games.
options.label: DSP Emulation Engine

options.info: Output latency in milliseconds. Lower is tighter but risks dropouts. Only active with backends that support latency control (OpenAL).
options.label: Audio Latency

options.info: Decode the stereo mix into 5.1 surround. Requires a backend that supports DPL2 (OpenAL) and DSP in LLE mode; otherwise inert.
options.label: Dolby Pro Logic II Decoder

options.info: Trade-off between CPU cost and surround-decode accuracy.
options.label: DPL2 Decoding Quality

options.info: Internal mixer buffer in milliseconds. Higher is smoother but adds delay between picture and sound.
options.label: Audio Buffer Size

options.info: Synthesize silence when emulation can't keep up. Disable for more accurate native behaviour; enable for smoothness.
options.label: Fill Audio Gaps

options.info: Time-stretch audio to keep pitch constant when emulation runs off 100%. Useful with fast-forward.
options.label: Preserve Audio Pitch

options.info: Silence audio while running unthrottled (fast-forward). Avoids pitch/playback artifacts.
options.label: Mute When Unthrottled

options.info: Master output volume.
options.label: Volume

options.info: Run CPU and GPU emulation on separate threads. Big speed gain; a few timing-sensitive games may glitch with it on.
options.label: Dual Core (Speed Hack)

options.info: Process AR/Gecko cheat codes. Off by default for safety.
options.label: Enable Cheats

options.info: Pre-load the entire disc image into RAM at boot. Eliminates disc I/O stutter; uses more host memory.
options.label: Load Whole Game Into Memory

options.info: Force a region's settings (language, video mode) regardless of disc region.
options.label: Allow Mismatched Region Settings

options.info: Cap on emulated speed relative to native. Unlimited removes the throttle.
options.label: Speed Limit

options.info: Region used for games whose region can't be auto-detected. Affects boot timing and the system-menu locale.
options.label: Fallback Region

options.info: The CPU backend. JIT is required for full-speed gameplay; the interpreters are debug/accuracy fallbacks.
options.label: CPU Emulation Engine

options.info: Emulate the memory management unit. Slower but required by a small set of games (typically Virtual Console / homebrew).
options.label: Enable MMU

options.info: Pause emulation when Dolphin reports a non-fatal error.
options.label: Pause on Panic

options.info: Emulate the CPU's L1 cache. Slower but more accurate; needed for a handful of self-modifying-code games.
options.label: Enable Write-Back Cache (Slow)

options.info: Compensate for accumulated frame-pacing drift over long sessions.
options.label: Correct Time Drift

options.info: Aggressively present frames as soon as they're ready. Lower latency, more tearing without V-Sync.
options.label: Rush Frame Presentation

options.info: Smooth pacing for frames that finish ahead of schedule.
options.label: Smooth Early Presentation

options.info: Allow the multiplier below to scale the emulated CPU clock. Some games run smoother overclocked; others crash.
options.label: Enable CPU Clock Override

options.info: Multiplier on the emulated CPU clock when overclocking is enabled. 1x = native.
options.label: CPU Overclock Multiplier

options.info: Scale the video-interface clock independently of the CPU. Affects refresh-rate timing for some games.
options.label: Enable VBI Frequency Override

options.info: Multiplier on the VI clock when VI overclocking is enabled.
options.label: VI Overclock Multiplier

options.info: Skip the GameCube boot animation and start the game directly. When off, requires IPL.bin in the BIOS folder.
options.label: Skip Main Menu (IPL)

options.info: System language used by GameCube games that respect it.
options.label: System Language

options.info: Device in the GameCube's left memory-card / EXI slot.
options.label: Slot A

options.info: Device in the GameCube's right memory-card / EXI slot.
options.label: Slot B

options.info: Device on the GameCube's serial port — network adapters in compatible games.
options.label: Serial Port 1 (SP1)

options.info: Make a USB keyboard visible to Wii software.
options.label: Connect USB Keyboard

options.info: Patch the Wii Shop / Channels to use community WiiLink servers. Off by default to avoid third-party network calls.
options.label: Enable WiiConnect24 (WiiLink)

options.info: Make a virtual SD card visible to Wii software. Required for save imports, channel installs, and SD-using homebrew.
options.label: Insert SD Card

options.info: When off, the SD card is read-only — protects a shared image from accidental modification.
options.label: Allow Writes to SD Card

options.info: Mirror the SD card image from a host folder.
options.label: Auto-Sync SD with Folder

options.info: Capacity of the virtual SD card. Auto uses the image file as-is.
options.label: SD Card Size
